title: Chonemorpha cristata
taxonomy: Chonemorpha cristata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Chonemorpha, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. The family Apocynaceae is a large group of flowering plants that includes several other genera such as Plumeria and Mandevilla. Chonemorpha cristata is further classified within the tribe Plumerieae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.

intro: Chonemorpha cristata, also known as the Crested Chonemorpha, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its attractive, fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Chonemorpha cristata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Chonemorpha cristata is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
